authors: De La Porte C, Van de Kelft E
title: Spinal cord stimulation in failed back surgery syndrome
study_question: Does uniform patient selection improve long-term pain control with spinal cord stimulation in failed back surgery syndrome?
population_assessed: 78 patients with failed back surgery syndrome without surgically correctable lesions, mean age 54 years.
outcome_measures: Pain relief grade rating, medication consumption, lifestyle changes, device continued use
inclusion_criteria: Proven FBSS without surgically treatable lesion, no major psychiatric pathology, excellent pain relief during trial stimulation.
answer_to_study_question: 54% achieved long-term success with at least 50% pain relief; 90% reduced medication; 61% improved lifestyle.
